steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The goal calls for an example, which requires naming a specific particle physicist and connecting them to the field — not discussing physics in general.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Neutrinos are a type of subatomic particle that particle physicists are still trying to understand.’: Describes the field without naming any specific physicist. Doesn't fulfill "example." ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Particle physicist Samoil M. Bilenky is known for his research on neutrino oscillations.’: A specific physicist is named and connected to neutrino research — exactly what an example requires. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘The research done on neutrino oscillations exemplifes the work particle physicists do to advance our understanding of subatomic particles.’: No specific physicist named. Too general. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘By studying neutrino oscillations—to name just one example—particle physicists can learn more about neutrinos.’: Same problem — general discussion, no named individual. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
